phase1.output:
Max attempts: 3
Value: 42
PI: 3.14159
Overridden successfully!
This method is final!
phase1.explanation:
The 'final' keyword prevents changes:

final variables: cannot be reassigned
• Must be initialized once
• Can be initialized in constructor
• Constants: static final

final methods: cannot be overridden
• Subclasses can't change the behavior
• Provides protection against accidental overriding

final classes: cannot be extended
• No subclasses allowed
• Used for immutable classes (String, Integer)

Common uses:
• public static final int MAX_SIZE = 100; — constants
• final class String — prevents inheritance
• final void run() — prevents overriding

final vs static:
• final: can't change value
• static: belongs to class (shared)

Immutable classes: all fields final, no setters, class final

phase2.errorMessage:
Test.java:6: error: cannot inherit from final Parent
class Child extends Parent {  // ERROR! Can't extend final class
                  ^
1 error
phase2.explanation:
Parent is declared final, so it can't be extended.

This is intentional — final classes are meant to be 'closed' for inheritance.

Common final classes in Java:
• String
• Integer, Double, etc. (wrapper classes)
• Math

If you need to use a final class, you can't create subclasses. Use composition instead.

phase3.prompt:
Create a class Config with:
• static final int MAX_USERS = 100
• static final String APP_NAME = "MyApp"
• final field: version (String) — set via constructor
• final method: displayVersion() — prints the version

In main, create a Config object and display everything.
dialogueHints: MAX_USERS and APP_NAME are constants (static final)
version is final but initialized in constructor
displayVersion is final — can't be overridden
